summary: Deployment evidence acceptance now fails closed unless the cryptographic verifier report is bound to the exact deployment-proof digest under evaluation.

problem.observed: build-evidence-verification-input.mjs emitted signatureSubjectVerification, but build-evidence-acceptance.mjs and the workflow's all-required invariant did not require that status to equal match.
problem.risk: A verified signature report naming different artifact bytes could be excluded from the final acceptance conjunction, creating inconsistent evidence semantics.

changes.effect: Adds signature.subject-mismatch as a rejecting reason when signatureSubjectVerification is absent or not match, records signature_subject status, and upgrades the derivation rule.
Adds explicit mismatch and missing-binding regression cases and aligns valid fixtures with the verification-input schema.
Requires signatureSubjectVerification.status=match in the final invariant and publishes the binding requirement in the run summary; the second commit corrects shell quoting.

falsification_routes: Run node --test cloudflare-static/build-evidence-acceptance.test.mjs on commit 68e357de6b3483e2b7c2cffe0327e4217a5570e0 or a descendant.
Construct a verification manifest with signatureVerification.status=verified and signatureSubjectVerification.status=mismatch; acceptance must reject with signature.subject-mismatch.
Dispatch the Cloudflare workflow after credentials exist and inspect cloudflare-deployment-acceptance.json.
limits: No Cloudflare credentials were available to this run.
No public Pages hostname or served identity was verified.
Cryptographic provenance does not establish scientific or medical truth.
privacy: No secrets, private chat, or health material are included.
